nodes.label: The Tapping Signal -- Two-Hand Tapping as Compositional Tool
nodes.description: Dave Knudson's two-hand tapping: both hands on the fretboard simultaneously, building entire song structures -- riffs, melodies, rhythmic patterns. Combined with digital delay and loop pedals for cascading layers. Structural, not ornamental. From Botch (Tacoma mathcore) aggression to Minus the Bear warmth. The technique is genre-agnostic; the application is everything. E=5.

nodes.label: Math Rock Enters the Engine -- Genre Stage 93
nodes.description: First math rock entry in 113 degrees of genre documentation. Complex time signatures (7/8, 11/8, alternating meters), angular riff structures, polyrhythmic drumming, technical virtuosity. Minus the Bear's innovation: making math rock accessible and danceable. Technical musicianship disguised as party music. Genre's late entry reflects its niche status.

nodes.label: Suicide Squeeze Records -- New Label in the Engine
nodes.description: Founded 1996 Seattle by David Dickenson. Initially limited-edition 7" singles label. Roster: Minus the Bear, Modest Mouse (early), Elliott Smith, Botch, Pretty Girls Make Graves, The Thermals. Joins Sub Pop, Kill Rock Stars, K Records, Up Records, Barsuk, Sargent House in the engine's label map. Eclectic -- no genre specialization, quality and independence as unifying principle.

nodes.label: Capitol Hill Streak Extends to Four (110-113)
nodes.description: Four consecutive Capitol Hill acts: Kinski (110, E=6), Helms Alee (111, E=6), 764-HERO (112, E=4), Minus the Bear (113, E=5). Energy: 6, 6, 4, 5. The neighborhood's diversity: noise rock, lo-fi indie/electronic, math rock/electronic. Four different genres, four different energy levels, all from Capitol Hill.

nodes.label: The Botch Connection -- Mathcore to Math Rock
nodes.description: Dave Knudson came from Botch (Tacoma mathcore). Botch was aggressive, angular, punishingly heavy -- closer to metalcore. Knudson brought the same tapping vocabulary but applied it to a completely different emotional palette: warm, melodic, danceable. Same hands, same fretboard, radically different music. Botch at E=6 aggression, Minus the Bear at E=5 pleasure.

nodes.label: Technical Musicianship Disguised as Party Music
nodes.description: Minus the Bear's signature: hooks, danceability, and lyrical hedonism wrapping complex time signatures and tapping patterns. The technical complexity is there if you listen for it but never demands attention. It supports the hooks rather than competing with them. Proved that math rock's perceived inaccessibility was a choice, not a requirement.

cross_references.relationship: CAPITOL HILL STREAK. Four consecutive (110-113). Lo-fi indie/electronic (E=4) to math rock/electronic (E=5). Energy rises one step. Both incorporate electronic elements on independent labels.
TECHNICAL MUSICIANSHIP. Both technically accomplished. Crushing harmonics (E=6) vs. tapping complexity (E=5). Different energy, shared commitment to pushing instruments to unusual places.
NOISE TO MATH ROCK SPECTRUM. E=6 noise vs. E=5 math rock/electronic. Both complex and unconventional. The spectrum from chaos to precision.
CAPITOL HILL INFRASTRUCTURE. Both Capitol Hill on independent labels. Barsuk vs. Suicide Squeeze. Same clubs, same scene, different genres.
BOTCH CONNECTION. Knudson from Botch; Kinski and Botch were PNW heavy/experimental contemporaries. Aggression to warmth parallels E=6 to E=5.
